About: It is about the preservation of established institutions and traditional principles within a political democracy, in combination with social and economic programmes designed to benefit the ordinary person.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with paternalistic conservatism.
It is associated with Tory democracy.
BelongsTo: It belongs to British political conservatism.
ConsistsOf: It consists of the preservation of established institutions and traditional principles within a political democracy, in combination with social and economic programmes designed to benefit the ordinary person.
CreatedBy: It was created by Benjamin Disraeli.
CreatedFor: It was created for working-class people.
CreatedTo: It was created to improve the lives of the people, provide social support and protect the working classes.
HasA: It has obligations towards each other.
It has a duty on the part of the rich to the poor and to the needy.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the belief that society should develop in an organic way.
It is characterized by the emphasis on social obligation rather than individualism.
It is characterized by a pragmatic and non-ideological approach.
It is characterized by the need for flexible policies.
It is characterized by seeking compromise with ideological opponents for the sake of social stability.
Influenced: It influenced the Conservative Party's tolerance of the Labour government's Keynesian intervention in the economy.
It influenced the formation of a welfare state.
It influenced the formation of the National Health Service.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the feudal concept of noblesse oblige.
IsA: It is a form of British political conservatism.
It is a variant of paternalistic conservatism.
It is a solution to the division of Britain into rich and poor nations.
KnownAs: It is known as Tory democracy.
KnownFor: It is known for advocating the preservation of established institutions and traditional principles within a political democracy, in combination with social and economic programmes designed to benefit the ordinary person.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the post-war consensus from 1945.
It occurred during the interwar period between 1919 and 1939.
It occurred during the early 1980s recession.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the first half of the 20th century.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the repercussions of the Industrial Revolution.
UsedFor: It is used for reconciling the interests of all social classes.
It is used for improving the conditions of the poor.
It is used for portraying the Liberal Party as selfish individualists.
It is used for concentrating welfare on those in need.
It is used for encouraging people to help themselves.
It is used for reversing national decline.
UsedIn: It is used in the Conservative Party manifesto.
UsedTo: It is used to benefit the ordinary person.
It is used to improve their lives via factory and health acts as well as greater protection for workers.
It is used to reconcile the interests of all social classes.
It is used to improve the lives of the people.
It is used to provide social support.
It is used to protect the working classes.
It is used to improve the conditions of the poor.
It is used to portray the Liberal Party as selfish individualists.
It is used to create a benevolent hierarchy.
